sourceMovie.title: Legend
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Legend (2015) as a crime theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Gangster Squad
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Gangster Squad (2013) operates in the same crime as Legend, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Sin City: A Dame to Kill For
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Sin City: A Dame to Kill For (2014) belongs to the same conversation as Legend — both anchor their crime in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Layer Cake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Legend's crime stayed with you, Layer Cake (2004) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: The Infiltrator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Legend excels in crime, The Infiltrator (2016) doubles down on thriller to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: The Client
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Client (1994) is the natural next step after Legend: same crime DNA, different perspective. The dark streak and thriller elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: The Dead Pool
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Legend who respond to its crime will find The Dead Pool (1988) immediately familiar. Both films share a thriller sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Legend fans keep landing on Henry: Portrait of a Serial Killer (1986) — the crime is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: City of Lies
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: City of Lies draws from the same crime well as Legend, channeling thriller in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2018 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Veronica Guerin
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Veronica Guerin (2003) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Legend later expanded. If Legend resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Gloria
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of Legend — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into Gloria (1980). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

faq.question: What should I watch after Legend?
faq.answer: Try Gangster Squad, Sin City: A Dame to Kill For, Layer Cake—each shares DNA with Legend while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Legend?
faq.answer: Legend (2015) is best described as Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Legend worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
